nl: All people who invite others to come to their ceremony make wedding plans.

nl: Anyone who has a well-attended wedding invites others to come to their ceremony.

nl: Some people that have well-attended weddings have larger families.
siv_canonical_fol: exists x.(exists y.((Person(x) & Have(x, y) & Wedding(y) & WellAttended(y))) & HaveLargerFamily(x))

nl: If John is engaged, then John does not invite friends to their ceremony and make wedding plans.
folio_gold_fol: Engaged(john) → ¬(∃y ∃z (¬(y=x) ∧ ¬(z=x) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ InviteToComeTo(john, y, wedding) ∧ Invite(john, z, comeTo, wedding))) ∧ Make(john, weddingPlan)

nl: If John has a larger family, then either John has a well-attended wedding or invites friends to their ceremony.
folio_gold_fol: Have(john, largerFamily) → Have(john, wellAttendedWedding) ⊕ ∃y ∃z (¬(y=x) ∧ ¬(z=x) ∧ ¬(y=z) ∧ Invite(x, y, comeTo, wedding) ∧ InviteToComeTo(x, z, wedding))
notes: normalized: Have(john, largerFamily) -> Have(john, wellAttendedWedding) exists y exists z (-(y=x) & -(z=x) & -(y=z) & Invite(x, y, comeTo, wedding) & InviteToComeTo(x, z, wedding))

nl: If a film is appropriate for people of all ages, then children can watch it without guidance from parents.

siv_canonical_fol: all x.(exists z.(Film(x)) -> ((ContainsExcessiveEroticContent(x) & ContainsViolentContent(x)) -> all y.(exists z.(Child(y)) -> -WatchWithoutGuidance(y, x))))

nl: Heck cattle were bred back in the 1920s to resemble the aurochs.

siv_canonical_fol: exists x.((Animal(x) & ToBeBredBack(x)) & exists y.(ExtinctAnimal(y) & Resemble(x, y)))
notes: normalized: exists x exists y (Animal(x) & Animal(y) & (-(x=y)) & BreedingBack(x) & BreedingBack(y) & (exists w(Dead(w) & Resemble(x, w)) & (-(w=z)) & (exists z(Dead(z) & Resemble(y, z))))
